name: Honolulu Beerworks
tagline: Kakaako's original craft brewery — industrial taproom pouring Hawaii-brewed beers with rotating food trucks outside.
mustOrder: Cocoweizen — coconut hefeweizen, a local favorite
seasonal IPA — whatever's on the rotating tap
tasting flight — try 4-5 beers at once
parking: Street parking on Coral St; Kakaako lots
talkStory: Founded in 2014 by Geoff Seideman, a California transplant who quit engineering to brew beer, Honolulu Beerworks was the catalyst for Kakaako's craft beer boom. The Cocoweizen -- a hefeweizen brewed with real toasted coconut -- became an instant local icon and won medals at the Great American Beer Festival. The converted warehouse with roll-up garage doors set the template that every Kakaako brewery copied.
